meta.title: Developer Discovers Rafters
meta.author: vault
meta.trigger: Maya's AI generates garbage UI for the third time this week

steps.title: The Frustration

layers.evidence: The generated code in her IDE. A screen that feels cluttered but she can't articulate why.
layers.customer_actions: Stares at the screen. Tries rearranging components. Asks the AI 'make this look better.' The AI reshuffles the same components. Nothing improves.
layers.frontstage: Nothing from Rafters. Maya does not know we exist.

pain_points: The AI has no design judgment. It rearranges without reasoning.
Maya's company has a design system wiki but pasting it into context is unreliable.
The design team is backlogged. She won't get a review for 3 days.

steps.title: The Search

layers.evidence: Google results, Twitter/Bluesky post, Brad Frost article, Slack message, Hacker News thread, conference talk recording
layers.customer_actions: Searches 'AI design system,' 'MCP design tools,' 'cognitive load components,' or 'make AI UI better.' Or clicks a link someone shared.
layers.frontstage: rafters.studio appears in results. The page title and meta description must answer 'what is this' in 5 seconds.
layers.backstage: SEO. Social presence. Brad Frost amplification. Community word-of-mouth. Conference presence.
layers.support: Search engine indexing. Social media algorithms. Community channels.

pain_points: The search space is noisy. 'AI design system' returns Figma AI, Galileo, v0, Uizard.
If the meta description says 'design intelligence protocol' without context, she won't click.
If she arrives from Brad Frost's blog, she's primed. Cold search, she's skeptical.

steps.title: The Landing Page

layers.evidence: rafters.studio landing page. The headline, the comparison table, the live tools.
layers.customer_actions: Reads the headline. Scans the page. Either clicks a tool or bounces.
layers.frontstage: 'Your AI doesn't have taste.' Problem statement she recognizes. Comparison table. Get started with two commands. Open source callout.
layers.backstage: Astro SSR on Cloudflare. Rafters components styling the page. Color intelligence API powering live tools.
layers.support: Cloudflare CDN. Edge caching. Analytics Engine.

pain_points: If the page looks like every other dev tool marketing site, she bounces.
If there's no proof on the page (just claims), she bounces.
If the comparison table oversells, she'll check and lose trust.

steps.title: The Live Tool

layers.evidence: Interactive tool on rafters.studio. Real data, real intelligence, real response.
layers.customer_actions: Color viewer: picks a color, sees name, emotional impact, cultural context, harmonies, accessibility. Budget calculator: selects components, sees cognitive load tally, gets told 'over budget' with suggestions.
layers.frontstage: The tool responds in under 500ms. Intelligence data is specific, not generic.
layers.backstage: buildColorValue() for local math. Color API with sync=true for intelligence. Client-side scoring for budget.
layers.support: Cloudflare Workers (color API). Vectorize (embedding cache). Workers AI (intelligence generation).

pain_points: Color API is slow or returns generic intelligence.
Budget calculator scores feel arbitrary.
The tool looks like every other color picker.
metrics: Click-through to Get Started after using a tool

steps.title: The Install

layers.evidence: Terminal output. pnpx rafters init progress messages. .rafters/ directory created. 240+ tokens generated.
layers.customer_actions: Runs pnpx rafters init. Watches output. Opens .rafters/ directory.
layers.frontstage: CLI detects framework. Generates tokens. Creates .rafters/ with token files. Prints summary with next step.
layers.backstage: Framework detection from package.json. Token generation from math-utils. Color API call. DTCG export.
layers.support: npm registry. Node.js runtime. Filesystem writes. Color API.

pain_points: pnpx rafters init fails. Node version, network, pnpm vs npm confusion.
Output is too verbose or too quiet.
Generated tokens are grayscale defaults. Must set expectations.
metrics: Immediate next action (mcp, studio, or abandon)

steps.title: The First MCP Query

layers.evidence: MCP server running. AI agent queries rafters_component or rafters_cognitive_budget. Response contains intelligence no other tool provides.
layers.customer_actions: Runs pnpx rafters mcp. Returns to IDE. Asks AI to build a component. AI queries MCP automatically. Maya sees intelligence in the AI's output.
layers.frontstage: The AI's output changes. Instead of generic Button, it generates with correct variant, references cognitive load score, notes attention economics rules, flags trust concerns.
layers.backstage: MCP server reads component intelligence from JSDoc metadata. Resolves token dependencies. Computes cognitive budget.
layers.support: MCP protocol (stdio transport). Node.js MCP server. Component registry. Token dependency graph.

pain_points: MCP server doesn't start. Most critical failure point.
The AI doesn't query MCP automatically. Manual config needed.
MCP response is shallow. Must contain scores, rules, patterns.
The AI ignores the MCP response.
metrics: Time from install to first MCP query

steps.title: The Share

layers.evidence: Slack message in #frontend. Screenshot of AI output with cognitive load scores. PR where design review finds zero issues.
layers.customer_actions: Posts in team Slack. Shares screenshot. Submits PR where the AI already checked composition budget.
layers.frontstage: Nothing from Rafters directly. Maya's experience IS the marketing.
layers.support: Slack, GitHub PR, team communication channels.

pain_points: Maya can't explain it quickly. 'Design intelligence protocol' means nothing to her manager.
The evidence isn't visual. JSON MCP response doesn't screenshot well.
The team doesn't care if Maya's PRs aren't noticeably better.
